title: Fold attention KV reducer inputs to reduce macro pin pressure
motivation: Flat and registered-tree attention KV reducers reached a boundary at p8/p16. The preserved evidence does not include a detailed OpenROAD failure stage, but repeated failure at 50% and 60% utilization is consistent with macro interface/pin pressure rather than only logic depth. A folded reducer lowers input bus width by accepting a smaller number of partials per cycle.
hypothesis: Keeping lanes fixed at l16 while reducing presented partials from p to partials_per_cycle will make p8 and p16 physically feasible, at the cost of ceil(partials / partials_per_cycle) cycles of reducer latency.

expected_behavior.functional: Exact signed value-lane sums and statistic sums match the full reducer over one group.
expected_behavior.timing_model: Reducer latency equals ceil(partials / partials_per_cycle) accepted chunks, plus output handshake effects.
expected_behavior.physical_model: Top-level value input width is partials_per_cycle * lanes * value_bits instead of partials * lanes * value_bits.

risks: If failures remain, pressure may come from output bus width or internal lane accumulators rather than only partial input pins.
The perf model must charge reducer cycles before using folded results in Llama7B schedule estimates.
